tasks.title: Create repository scaffold and metadata
tasks.description: Create repository.json and top-level README.md for Home Assistant addon repository
tasks.details:
Create repository.json with Home Assistant addon repository format:
```json
{
  "name": "Home Assistant MCP Addons",
  "url": "https://github.com/[username]/ha-mcp-addons",
  "maintainer": "[maintainer name]"
}
```

Create README.md describing:
- Purpose: Four MCP server addons extending ha-mcp functionality
- List all four addons: mcp-filesystem, mcp-git, mcp-node-red, mcp-influxdb
- Installation instructions (Settings → Add-ons → Add-on Store → Repositories)
- Brief description of each addon's capabilities
- Architecture note: HTTP/SSE MCP servers for local Open WebUI consumption
- Compatibility: aarch64 (Home Assistant Yellow/CM4)
tasks.testStrategy: Validate repository.json is valid JSON with required keys (name, url, maintainer). Verify README.md provides clear installation and usage instructions for all four addons.

tasks.title: Implement mcp-filesystem addon
tasks.description: Build complete mcp-filesystem addon wrapping @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem as HTTP/SSE server
tasks.details:
Create mcp-filesystem/ directory with:

**config.yaml:**
- name: MCP Filesystem Server
- version: 1.0.0
- slug: mcp-filesystem
- description: MCP server for /config filesystem access
- arch: [aarch64]
- map: ["config:rw"]
- options:
  - port: 3001 (int)
  - allowed_paths: ["/config"] (list)
- ports: {3001/tcp: 3001}

**package.json:**
- dependencies: @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk (or supergateway for HTTP bridge)

**Dockerfile:**
- FROM node:20-alpine
- WORKDIR /app
- COPY package*.json ./
- RUN npm ci --only=production
- COPY run.sh /
- RUN chmod +x /run.sh
- CMD ["/run.sh"]

**run.sh:**
- Parse config JSON from stdin
- Extract port and allowed_paths
- Start HTTP/SSE wrapper around server-filesystem
- Bind to 0.0.0.0:${port}
- Pass allowed_paths as arguments

**README.md:**
- Purpose, configuration options, usage examples
tasks.testStrategy: Validate config.yaml against HA addon schema. Validate package.json syntax. Verify Dockerfile uses node:20-alpine (aarch64 compatible). Check run.sh correctly parses addon options and binds to configurable port.

tasks.title: Implement mcp-git addon
tasks.description: Build complete mcp-git addon with custom MCP server for git operations
tasks.details:
Create mcp-git/ directory with:

**config.yaml:**
- name: MCP Git Server
- version: 1.0.0
- slug: mcp-git
- description: MCP server for git operations
- arch: [aarch64]
- map: ["config:rw"]
- options:
  - port: 3002 (int)
  - repo_path: "/config" (str)
  - ssh_key_path: "/config/.ssh/id_rsa" (str)
- ports: {3002/tcp: 3002}

**Implementation choice: Node.js + simple-git**

**package.json:**
- dependencies: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, simple-git, express (for HTTP/SSE)

**src/server.js:**
- Import MCP SDK Server, SSEServerTransport
- Import simple-git
- Implement MCP tools:
  - git_status: simpleGit.status()
  - git_diff: simpleGit.diff()
  - git_add: simpleGit.add('-A')
  - git_commit(message): simpleGit.commit(message)
  - git_push: simpleGit.env({GIT_SSH_COMMAND: `ssh -i ${ssh_key_path} -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no`}).push()
  - git_log(n=10): simpleGit.log({maxCount: n})
- Start HTTP/SSE server on configured port

**Dockerfile:**
- FROM node:20-alpine
- RUN apk add --no-cache git openssh-client
- WORKDIR /app
- COPY package*.json ./
- RUN npm ci --only=production
- COPY src/ ./src/
- COPY run.sh /
- RUN chmod +x /run.sh
- CMD ["/run.sh"]

**run.sh:**
- Parse config from stdin
- Extract options
- Execute: node src/server.js

**README.md:**
- Purpose, configuration, tool descriptions, SSH key setup
tasks.testStrategy: Validate config.yaml schema. Validate package.json syntax. Verify Dockerfile installs git and ssh-client. Review src/server.js implements all 6 git tools with correct MCP tool schema and GIT_SSH_COMMAND environment variable for git_push.

tasks.title: Implement mcp-node-red addon
tasks.description: Build complete mcp-node-red addon with custom MCP server for Node-RED HTTP API
tasks.details:
Create mcp-node-red/ directory with:

**config.yaml:**
- name: MCP Node-RED Server
- version: 1.0.0
- slug: mcp-node-red
- description: MCP server for Node-RED flow management
- arch: [aarch64]
- options:
  - port: 3003 (int)
  - node_red_url: "http://localhost:1880" (str)
  - node_red_user: "" (str)
  - node_red_password: "" (str)
- ports: {3003/tcp: 3003}

**Implementation choice: Python + requests**

**requirements.txt:**
- mcp>=1.0.0
- requests>=2.31.0

**src/server.py:**
- Import mcp.server, mcp.server.stdio (convert to HTTP/SSE)
- Import requests
- Implement authentication: if user/password provided, POST /auth/token to get bearer token
- Store token in session, include in headers
- Implement MCP tools:
  - list_flows: GET /flows, extract [{id, label, type}]
  - get_flow(id): GET /flow/{id}
  - update_flow(id, flow_json): PUT /flow/{id}
  - deploy(type='full'): POST /flows with Node-RED-Deployment-Type header
  - inject_node(node_id): POST /inject/{node_id}
  - get_nodes: GET /nodes
- Start HTTP/SSE MCP server on configured port

**Dockerfile:**
- FROM python:3.11-slim
- WORKDIR /app
- COPY requirements.txt ./
- RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
- COPY src/ ./src/
- COPY run.sh /
- RUN chmod +x /run.sh
- CMD ["/run.sh"]

**run.sh:**
- Parse config from stdin
- Execute: python src/server.py

**README.md:**
- Purpose, configuration, tool descriptions, authentication notes
tasks.testStrategy: Validate config.yaml schema. Validate requirements.txt syntax. Review src/server.py implements all 6 Node-RED tools with correct HTTP endpoints and authentication flow (POST /auth/token if credentials provided, bearer token in subsequent requests).

tasks.title: Implement mcp-influxdb addon
tasks.description: Build complete mcp-influxdb addon with custom MCP server for InfluxDB 1.8.x HTTP API
tasks.details:
Create mcp-influxdb/ directory with:

**config.yaml:**
- name: MCP InfluxDB Server
- version: 1.0.0
- slug: mcp-influxdb
- description: MCP server for InfluxDB 1.8.x queries
- arch: [aarch64]
- options:
  - port: 3004 (int)
  - influxdb_url: "http://localhost:8086" (str)
  - influxdb_database: "homeassistant" (str)
  - influxdb_user: "" (str)
  - influxdb_password: "" (str)
- ports: {3004/tcp: 3004}

**Implementation: Python + requests (raw HTTP calls)**

**requirements.txt:**
- mcp>=1.0.0
- requests>=2.31.0

**src/server.py:**
- Import mcp.server (convert to HTTP/SSE)
- Import requests
- Build auth tuple: (user, password) if provided
- Implement MCP tools:
  - query(q, database=default): GET /query?db={database}&q={q}, parse JSON response
  - list_measurements(database=default): query("SHOW MEASUREMENTS", database)
  - list_databases(): query("SHOW DATABASES")
  - get_recent(measurement, field, n=10, database=default): query(f"SELECT {field} FROM {measurement} ORDER BY time DESC LIMIT {n}", database), format as [{time, value}]
- Start HTTP/SSE MCP server on configured port

**Dockerfile:**
- FROM python:3.11-slim
- WORKDIR /app
- COPY requirements.txt ./
- RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
- COPY src/ ./src/
- COPY run.sh /
- RUN chmod +x /run.sh
- CMD ["/run.sh"]

**run.sh:**
- Parse config from stdin
- Execute: python src/server.py

**README.md:**
- Purpose, configuration, tool descriptions, InfluxQL examples
tasks.testStrategy: Validate config.yaml schema. Validate requirements.txt syntax. Review src/server.py implements all 4 InfluxDB tools using raw HTTP calls (GET /query, GET /ping) with auth tuple, no influxdb client library dependency.

tasks.title: Create package.json for mcp-filesystem
tasks.description: Create package.json with dependencies for mcp-filesystem HTTP/SSE bridge
tasks.details:
Create mcp-filesystem/package.json:
```json
{
  "name": "mcp-filesystem-addon",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "dependencies": {
    "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem": "^0.5.0",
    "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "^0.5.0"
  }
}
```

Implementation note: Use @modelcontextprotocol/sdk's SSEServerTransport to wrap the stdio-based server-filesystem as an HTTP/SSE endpoint. Create a wrapper script that:
1. Imports server-filesystem's createServer
2. Creates SSE transport bound to 0.0.0.0:${port}
3. Connects stdio server to SSE transport
4. Passes allowed_paths from addon config
tasks.testStrategy: Validate package.json is syntactically correct JSON. Verify dependencies match latest stable @modelcontextprotocol packages. Confirm type: module for ES modules support.

tasks.title: Create stdio-to-HTTP bridge wrapper for mcp-filesystem
tasks.description: Implement bridge script converting server-filesystem stdio to HTTP/SSE
tasks.testStrategy: Verify bridge.js correctly spawns server-filesystem subprocess, pipes stdio to SSE transport, and binds HTTP server to 0.0.0.0:PORT. Confirm ALLOWED_PATHS environment variable is parsed and passed as arguments.

tasks.title: Validate all config.yaml files against Home Assistant addon schema
tasks.description: Ensure all four config.yaml files conform to HA addon schema requirements
tasks.details:
Required config.yaml structure for each addon:
```yaml
name: <addon name>
version: 1.0.0
slug: <unique-slug>
description: <short description>
arch:
  - aarch64
ports:
  <port>/tcp: <port>
options:
  port: <default_port>
  # addon-specific options
schema:
  port: int
  # addon-specific schema
```

Additionally for mcp-filesystem and mcp-git:
```yaml
map:
  - config:rw
```

Validation checklist:
1. All required keys present: name, version, slug, description, arch, ports
2. arch includes aarch64
3. Port numbers unique across addons (3001-3004)
4. options and schema sections match
5. map: ["config:rw"] for filesystem and git addons
6. Valid YAML syntax (no tabs, correct indentation)
7. String values quoted where necessary
8. List/array syntax correct

Run YAML validation on all four config.yaml files.
tasks.testStrategy: Parse each config.yaml with a YAML validator. Verify no syntax errors. Check all required keys exist. Confirm arch contains aarch64. Verify port mappings are unique (3001, 3002, 3003, 3004). Ensure schema matches options structure.

tasks.title: Validate all JSON files for syntax correctness
tasks.description: Validate repository.json and all package.json files are syntactically correct
tasks.details:
Files to validate:
1. repository.json (root)
2. mcp-filesystem/package.json
3. mcp-git/package.json
4. mcp-node-red/requirements.txt (not JSON, but verify format)
5. mcp-influxdb/requirements.txt (not JSON, but verify format)

JSON validation:
- Parse with JSON.parse() or equivalent
- Check for trailing commas
- Verify all strings properly quoted
- Ensure no syntax errors

Requirements.txt validation:
- One dependency per line
- Format: package>=version or package==version
- No extra whitespace or comments breaking syntax

Use:
```bash
# JSON validation
jq empty repository.json
jq empty mcp-filesystem/package.json
jq empty mcp-git/package.json

# Python requirements validation
pip check requirements.txt (or manual inspection)
```
tasks.testStrategy: Run jq or JSON.parse on repository.json and all package.json files. Verify exit code 0 (no syntax errors). Manually inspect requirements.txt files for correct pip format (package>=version per line).

tasks.title: Create comprehensive test validation script
tasks.description: Create automated validation script to check all JSON, YAML, and file structure requirements
tasks.details:
Create validate.sh script in repository root:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "Validating repository structure..."

# Check required files exist
for file in repository.json README.md; do
  [ -f "$file" ] || { echo "Missing: $file"; exit 1; }
done

# Check each addon directory
for addon in mcp-filesystem mcp-git mcp-node-red mcp-influxdb; do
  echo "Checking $addon..."
  [ -d "$addon" ] || { echo "Missing directory: $addon"; exit 1; }
  [ -f "$addon/config.yaml" ] || { echo "Missing: $addon/config.yaml"; exit 1; }
  [ -f "$addon/Dockerfile" ] || { echo "Missing: $addon/Dockerfile"; exit 1; }
  [ -f "$addon/run.sh" ] || { echo "Missing: $addon/run.sh"; exit 1; }
  [ -f "$addon/README.md" ] || { echo "Missing: $addon/README.md"; exit 1; }
done

# Validate JSON files
echo "Validating JSON files..."
jq empty repository.json
[ -f mcp-filesystem/package.json ] && jq empty mcp-filesystem/package.json
[ -f mcp-git/package.json ] && jq empty mcp-git/package.json

# Validate YAML files
echo "Validating YAML files..."
for yaml in mcp-*/config.yaml; do
  python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('$yaml'))" || { echo "Invalid YAML: $yaml"; exit 1; }
done

# Check for aarch64 in all config.yaml
echo "Checking aarch64 architecture..."
for yaml in mcp-*/config.yaml; do
  grep -q "aarch64" "$yaml" || { echo "Missing aarch64: $yaml"; exit 1; }
done

# Check unique ports
echo "Checking unique ports..."
ports=$(grep -h "port:" mcp-*/config.yaml | sort | uniq -d)
[ -z "$ports" ] || { echo "Duplicate ports found"; exit 1; }

echo "All validations passed!"
```

Make executable: chmod +x validate.sh
tasks.testStrategy: Run ./validate.sh and verify exit code 0. Script should check: all required files exist, JSON syntax is valid, YAML syntax is valid, aarch64 is declared in all addons, ports are unique across addons (3001-3004).
